title: Listening - Part 1
description: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new city tour they went on.
extracts.audio_script: Man: That 'Hidden Alleys' tour was quite an eye-opener. I’ve lived here five years and never knew that courtyard existed. || Woman: [1]I found the guide's constant jokes a bit tiresome after the first hour[/1], to be honest. It felt like he was trying too hard to be an entertainer. || Man: I see what you mean. Some of the puns were a bit cringeworthy. || Woman: [2]What I really appreciated, though, was the high-quality wireless headsets they provided[/2]. It was so nice to hear every word without having to crowd around him. || Man: I agree. It made the whole experience much more relaxed. || Woman: Exactly. It’s a great concept for a tour, I just wish they’d stick to the facts a bit more.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That 'Hidden Alleys' tour was quite an eye-opener. I’ve lived here five years and never knew that courtyard existed.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the guide's constant jokes a bit tiresome after the first hour, to be honest. It felt like he was trying too hard to be an entertainer.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I see what you mean. Some of the puns were a bit cringeworthy.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: What I really appreciated, though, was the high-quality wireless headsets they provided. It was so nice to hear every word without having to crowd around him.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It made the whole experience much more relaxed.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a great concept for a tour, I just wish they’d stick to the facts a bit more.

questions.question: What did the woman find annoying about the tour guide?

options.A: his lack of knowledge about local history
options.C: his inability to manage the group size

questions.explanation: She mentions the 'constant jokes' were tiresome and he was 'trying too hard to be an entertainer' [1].

questions.question: Both speakers were impressed by the

options.A: audio technology used on the tour.

questions.explanation: They both praise the 'high-quality wireless headsets' which allowed them to hear clearly [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a new office layout.
extracts.audio_script: Man: So, what do you think of the 'open-plan' redesign? It’s certainly brighter in here now. || Woman: [3]I suspect the real motive was to squeeze more desks into the same floor space[/3], rather than fostering 'collaboration'. || Man: It does feel a bit cramped. I keep bumping into your chair. || Woman: [4]I’ve noticed that the noise levels have skyrocketed since they took down the partitions[/4]. I can hear every single phone call from the finance team. || Man: I agree. It’s impossible to focus on a complex report with all that background chatter. || Woman: Exactly. They’ve sacrificed our concentration just to save a few pounds on rent.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So, what do you think of the 'open-plan' redesign? It’s certainly brighter in here now.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suspect the real motive was to squeeze more desks into the same floor space, rather than fostering 'collaboration'.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It does feel a bit cramped. I keep bumping into your chair.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ve noticed that the noise levels have skyrocketed since they took down the partitions. I can hear every single phone call from the finance team.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s impossible to focus on a complex report with all that background chatter.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. They’ve sacrificed our concentration just to save a few pounds on rent.

questions.question: The woman believes the office redesign was primarily done to

options.A: improve the aesthetic appeal of the space.
options.B: increase the occupancy density of the room.

questions.explanation: She suspects the motive was to 'squeeze more desks into the same floor space' [3].

questions.question: What is the speakers' main complaint about the new layout?

options.A: the poor quality of the new furniture
options.B: the excessive amount of auditory distraction
options.C: the lack of natural light in the workspace

questions.explanation: They both agree that 'noise levels have skyrocketed' and it's 'impossible to focus' [4].

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a new smart-watch they have both bought.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I’m still exploring all the features on the 'Vertex' watch. The heart-rate monitor seems very responsive. || Woman: It is. But [5]I found the sleep-tracking data to be quite inconsistent[/5]. It said I was awake for two hours last night when I know I was out cold. || Man: I had a similar issue. I think it gets confused if you move in your sleep. || Woman: [6]I did think the haptic feedback for notifications was brilliantly subtle, though[/6]. It’s much nicer than having a phone vibrate loudly on the desk. || Man: I agree. It’s a very discreet way to stay connected during meetings. || Woman: Definitely. It’s a solid piece of tech, even if the software still needs a bit of fine-tuning.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I’m still exploring all the features on the 'Vertex' watch. The heart-rate monitor seems very responsive.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It is. But I found the sleep-tracking data to be quite inconsistent. It said I was awake for two hours last night when I know I was out cold.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I had a similar issue. I think it gets confused if you move in your sleep.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I did think the haptic feedback for notifications was brilliantly subtle, though. It’s much nicer than having a phone vibrate loudly on the desk.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s a very discreet way to stay connected during meetings.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Definitely. It’s a solid piece of tech, even if the software still needs a bit of fine-tuning.

questions.question: What is the woman's main criticism of the watch's performance?

options.A: the slow response time of the heart monitor
options.B: the inaccuracy of the health monitoring data
options.C: the short life of the internal battery

questions.explanation: She found the 'sleep-tracking data to be quite inconsistent' and incorrect about her being awake [5].

questions.question: Both speakers appreciate the watch because of its
questions.explanation: They both praise the 'haptic feedback' (vibrations) as being 'subtle' and 'discreet' [6].
